name: Holo Spaces
summary: Walk into living, shareable, infinitely nestable rooms of holo apps — each rendered from its κ address. A Space is itself one content κ: compose by dragging things in, fork with a gesture, share by the κ alone. The OS is already a sandbox; this reveals it.

description.p: Rooms.xyz needs a voxel library because it starts empty. Hologram does not — every app and object is already a live κ. So a room is just a curated, nestable arrangement of κ-addressed things, and a Space is itself a κ.

Holo Spaces composes by reference: members are κs, never copies. Each app member is mounted through the same projection every app boots through (holospace.html), re-derived before it renders (Law L5). A member can itself be a Space — so worlds nest into worlds without limit, mounted lazily and concurrently.

ul: A Space is one immutable content κ — the ordered member κs + layout + accent + mood
Open a κ → the exact arrangement re-derives; tamper a member → it refuses, honest empty slot
Drag any κ in to arrange; fork mints a new κ you can share by the κ alone (serverless link)
Infinite nesting: a Space is just another κ thing, mounted lazily when revealed

releases.description: The spine: a Space is a content κ. Compose-by-reference rooms of κ-addressed apps, mounted through the projection and re-derived (L5); infinite lazy+concurrent nesting (a Space as a member of a Space); drag-to-arrange + fork-to-a-new-κ; serverless share by κ. Fail-closed: an unresolvable or tampered member renders an honest empty slot, never a broken mount.
